title: How One Mom Built Financial Independence After Divorce: The Corporate Mom Story
guestBio: Tiana Reeves is a PMP-certified project manager, Six Sigma Black Belt, author, and creator of the Corporate Mom Movement. After navigating pregnancy loss, divorce, and corporate burnout, she reinvented herself and now helps other working mothers build financial literacy and career confidence.
excerpt: From pregnancy loss and divorce to PMP-certified powerhouse, Tiana Reeves shares her blueprint for working mom reinvention. Discover why marriage is like a corporate merger and how to teach your kids financial literacy early.
metaDescription: Working mom Tiana Reeves shares her journey from divorce to financial independence, plus how to teach kids money skills and build career confidence.
keyTakeaways: Marriage is like a corporate merger—make sure your cultures align before you combine companies.
Rich isn't about income—it's about joy, peace, and the memories you create with your family.
Start teaching children financial literacy early using the three-bucket system: savings, giving, and spending.
Imposter syndrome often signals you haven't found your true purpose yet, not that you're unqualified.
Financial dependence has hidden costs that go far beyond money—it affects your voice and choices.
Start with what you enjoy and are naturally good at—purpose and financial success will follow.
Peace in your household is worth more than gold and should be prioritized above material success.

pullQuotes.text: Marriage is a corporate merger—make sure your corporate cultures align before you merge.
Peace is worth more than gold. I would rather have peace in my household than anything else.
Your kids think you're a superhero—don't let anyone convince you otherwise.

content:
Sometimes the most powerful transformations come from our most painful seasons. That's exactly what happened to Tiana Reeves, who went from pregnancy loss, corporate burnout, and divorce to becoming a PMP-certified powerhouse and the creator of the Corporate Mom Movement. Her story isn't just about professional success—it's about completely redefining what it means to be rich, powerful, and free.

In this raw and inspiring conversation with Aimee Rickabus, Tiana shares the blueprint that took her from financial dependence to building her own empire, all while raising two children and teaching them the money skills she wishes she'd learned earlier. This isn't your typical success story—it's a masterclass in reinvention that every working mother needs to hear.

When Life Doesn't Go According to Plan

Tiana's transformation didn't happen overnight. In her twenties, she had what looked like the perfect ten-year plan mapped out. Then life threw her a series of curveballs that would have knocked most people down permanently. Pregnancy loss shattered her timeline. Corporate layoffs disrupted her career trajectory. A failing marriage forced her to confront uncomfortable truths about financial dependence.

But here's what makes Tiana's story so powerful: she didn't just survive these setbacks—she used them as fuel for complete reinvention. Every failure became a building block for resilience, teaching her that sometimes the best thing that can happen to you is having your original plan fall apart completely.

The Birth of the Corporate Mom Movement

The Corporate Mom Movement wasn't born from a business plan—it emerged from Tiana's realization that ambitious mothers are often made to feel uncomfortable about their drive and success. Society has conditioned us to believe that wanting more, earning more, and achieving more somehow makes us less nurturing or present as mothers. Tiana refuses to accept this narrative.

Your kids think you're a superhero, and Tiana believes we should stop letting anyone convince us otherwise. When you show your children what it looks like to build something meaningful, to recover from setbacks, and to create financial security, you're not taking away from your motherhood—you're modeling what strength and resilience actually look like.

Redefining What Rich Really Means

One of the most profound shifts in Tiana's journey was unlearning toxic beliefs about money and success. She had to challenge the narrative that financial ambition was somehow unfeminine or that wanting more made her greedy. Rich isn't about income, she learned. It's about joy, peace, and the memories you create with your family.

This redefinition allowed her to pursue financial independence not from a place of scarcity or desperation, but from a desire to create options and security for her family. When you understand that wealth is ultimately about freedom and peace of mind, the journey toward building it becomes less about proving yourself and more about creating the life you actually want.

Teaching Financial Literacy From Day One

Tiana isn't just building wealth—she's ensuring her children understand money from an early age. Using Dave Ramsey's method, she's implemented a three-bucket system for her kids: savings, giving, and spending. This isn't just about allowances—it's about teaching them that money is a tool with multiple purposes and that financial literacy is a life skill as essential as reading or writing.

The conversations you have with your children about money by age eight will shape their relationship with finances for life. Tiana understands this, which is why she's intentional about making money discussions normal, healthy, and empowering rather than stressful or shameful.

The Plot Twist That Changed Everything

Perhaps the most remarkable part of Tiana's story is how she achieved her PMP certification in just six weeks during the most challenging season of her life. While dealing with anxiety, depression, and a crumbling marriage, she could have easily given up on professional development. Instead, she doubled down on investing in herself.

This certification became more than just a credential—it was proof to herself that she could succeed independently. Sometimes the biggest breakthroughs happen when we're already at our breaking point, and Tiana's story is living proof that your hardest season can also become your most transformative one.

Marriage as a Corporate Merger

One of Tiana's most insightful perspectives is viewing marriage through a business lens. Marriage is a corporate merger, she explains, and divorce is a divestiture. Just like any successful merger, you need to ensure your corporate cultures align before you combine companies.

This framework removes the emotion and fantasy from partner selection and forces you to ask practical questions: Do our values align? Are our financial goals compatible? Can we build something stronger together than we could apart? When you're choosing a partner as a grown woman, you're essentially interviewing someone for a role at your life's company.

The True Cost of Financial Dependence

Tiana's journey revealed the hidden costs of financial dependence that extend far beyond money. When you're financially dependent, you lose your voice in decisions, your ability to leave unhealthy situations, and often your sense of self-worth. Financial independence isn't just about having money—it's about having choices.

The path to independence wasn't easy, but what she gained on the other side was invaluable: the ability to prioritize peace over everything else. As she puts it, peace is worth more than gold. When you can walk away from situations, people, or opportunities that compromise your peace, you've achieved a level of wealth that money alone can't buy.

Your Permission Slip to Start

Tiana's advice for women feeling stuck or unfulfilled is beautifully simple: start with what you enjoy. You don't need to have your entire purpose figured out before you begin. In fact, that feeling of imposter syndrome might not mean you're a fraud—it might mean you haven't found your true purpose yet.

Start with what brings you joy and energy. Build from there. Purpose and financial success will follow when you're operating from a place of authentic passion rather than trying to force yourself into someone else's definition of success.

Tiana Reeves' story is a powerful reminder that reinvention is possible at any stage of life, that your children are watching and learning from your resilience, and that the most important wealth you can build is the peace and freedom to live life on your own terms. Your superhero cape is waiting—it's time to put it on.
